# expo-localized-widgets

> Localization helpers for `expo-widgets` and an Expo config plugin that localizes iOS widget gallery metadata (the name and description shown in the iOS widget picker).

This is an independent community package. It is not created, maintained or endorsed by Expo or Software Mansion.

## What it does

`expo-widgets` (the official Expo SDK package for iOS WidgetKit) does not provide built-in localization support. This package fills that gap with two independent utilities:

1. **Runtime helper** (`localizeWidgetProps`) — resolves the correct locale and passes translations to a widget via `updateSnapshot` / `updateTimeline`.
2. **Config plugin** (`expo-localized-widgets`) — generates a `Localizable.xcstrings` string catalog inside the widget extension target at prebuild time, enabling localized `displayName` and `description` in the iOS widget gallery.

The package is RTL-ready. Passing the resolved locale into SwiftUI's `environment` modifier enables locale-aware layout for languages such as Arabic.

## Installation

```bash
npx expo install expo-widgets
npm install expo-localized-widgets
```

`expo-widgets` is iOS-only, requires a development build, and is not available in Expo Go.

## Runtime API

### `localizeWidgetProps(props, options)`

Merges widget data with the resolved locale and translations. Returns `props` extended with `widgetLocale: string` and `widgetTranslations: Record<string, string>`.

```ts
import { localizeWidgetProps } from 'expo-localized-widgets';

const props = localizeWidgetProps(
  { reservationCount: 3 },
  {
    locale: i18n.resolvedLanguage, // string | null | undefined
    fallbackLocale: 'en',
    translations: {
      en: { title: 'Next stay' },
      ar: { title: 'الإقامة القادمة' },
    },
  },
);

MyWidget.updateSnapshot(props);
// props.widgetLocale      → 'ar'
// props.widgetTranslations → { title: 'الإقامة القادمة' }
// props.reservationCount  → 3  (preserved unchanged)
```

Locale resolution order: exact match → language-only match (e.g. `en-US` → `en`) → `fallbackLocale`. Accepts both `en-US` and `en_US` forms.

### `getWidgetTranslations(options)`

Same options as `localizeWidgetProps`. Returns `{ widgetLocale, widgetTranslations }` without merging into existing props.

### `resolveWidgetLocale(locale, availableLocales, fallbackLocale)`

Low-level locale picker. Returns the best matching locale string from `availableLocales`. Throws when `fallbackLocale` is not in `availableLocales`.

### Using translations inside the widget

```tsx
import { VStack, Text } from '@expo/ui/swift-ui';
import { environment } from '@expo/ui/swift-ui/modifiers';

const MyWidget = (props) => {
  'widget';
  return (
    <VStack modifiers={[environment({ key: 'locale', value: props.widgetLocale })]}>
      <Text>{props.widgetTranslations.title}</Text>
    </VStack>
  );
};
```

## Config plugin

Register the plugin **after** `expo-widgets`. Source locale values must exactly match the `displayName` and `description` values in the `expo-widgets` config. The plugin warns at prebuild time when a mismatch is detected.

### Single target (flat form)

```ts
// app.config.ts
export default {
  expo: {
    plugins: [
      ['expo-widgets', {
        widgets: [{
          name: 'NextReservationWidget',
          displayName: 'Next reservation',
          description: 'Counts down to your next stay.',
          supportedFamilies: ['systemSmall'],
        }],
      }],
      ['expo-localized-widgets', {
        // targetName defaults to 'ExpoWidgetsTarget' (currently the only value expo-widgets supports)
        sourceLanguage: 'en',
        localizations: {
          en: { displayName: 'Next reservation', description: 'Counts down to your next stay.' },
          ar: { displayName: 'الحجز القادم', description: 'يعرض العد التنازلي لإقامتك القادمة.' },
        },
      }],
    ],
  },
};
```

### Multiple targets (array form)

```ts
['expo-localized-widgets', {
  targets: [
    {
      targetName: 'ExpoWidgetsTarget',
      sourceLanguage: 'en',
      localizations: {
        en: { displayName: 'Next reservation', description: 'Counts down to your next stay.' },
        ar: { displayName: 'الحجز القادم', description: 'يعرض العد التنازلي لإقامتك القادمة.' },
      },
    },
    {
      targetName: 'StatsWidgetTarget',
      sourceLanguage: 'en',
      localizations: {
        en: { displayName: 'Trip stats', description: 'Shows your travel summary.' },
        ar: { displayName: 'إحصائيات الرحلة', description: 'يعرض ملخص رحلاتك.' },
      },
    },
  ],
}]
```

## Config plugin options

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `targets` | `TargetOptions[]` | — | Array form for multiple extension targets. When set, top-level `targetName`/`sourceLanguage`/`localizations` are ignored. |
| `targetName` | `string` | `'ExpoWidgetsTarget'` | Xcode extension target name. `expo-widgets` currently fixes this value; option is future-ready. |
| `sourceLanguage` | `string` | `'en'` | Source locale. Values must exactly match the `displayName`/`description` in `expo-widgets`. |
| `localizations` | `object` | — | Required (flat form). Map of locale → key → translated string. |

Each `TargetOptions` entry in `targets` accepts the same `targetName`, `sourceLanguage`, and `localizations` fields with the same defaults.

## TypeScript

The package ships TypeScript types for both the runtime (`dist/index.d.ts`) and the config plugin (`plugin/withLocalizedWidgets.d.ts`). The plugin types are exposed via the `./app.plugin` export condition in `package.json`.

```ts
import type withLocalizedWidgets from 'expo-localized-widgets/app.plugin';
// withLocalizedWidgets.WithLocalizedWidgetsOptions
// withLocalizedWidgets.TargetOptions
// withLocalizedWidgets.ResolvedTarget
```

## Versioning

Package majors follow Expo SDK majors. Breaking changes within a major use the minor version; fixes use the patch version.

| Package | Expo SDK | expo-widgets |
|---|---|---|
| 56.x | 56.x | 56.x |
